title: rubus tomentosus var. glanduliramus
intro: Rubus tomentosus var. glanduliramus, also known as the glandular raspberry, is a type of wild raspberry plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). It is native to parts of Asia and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is rich in vitamins, minerals, and other nutrients that provide various health benefits, such as improving cardiovascular health, boosting the immune system, and reducing the risk of certain cancers. The leaves and stems of Rubus tomentosus var. glanduliramus can be prepared as teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal use, and can also be consumed as a raw vegetable or added to salads for a nutrient-rich snack.
